famous_foods.name: Litti Chokha
famous_foods.description: A quintessential Bihari dish consisting of roasted or baked whole wheat dough balls stuffed with sattu (roasted gram flour) and served with chokha (a mashed vegetable mix of roasted eggplant, potato, tomato, and spices).

famous_foods.name: Sattu Paratha
famous_foods.description: A popular flatbread made from whole wheat flour, typically stuffed with spiced sattu (roasted gram flour) and often served with chutney, pickle, or chokha.

famous_foods.name: Dal Pitha
famous_foods.description: Savory rice flour dumplings, often shaped like crescent moons, filled with spiced lentil paste and either steamed or fried. It's a flavorful and comforting dish.

famous_foods.name: Chana Ghugni
famous_foods.description: A spicy and tangy curry made from black chickpeas (kala chana), often served as a snack or a side dish. It's flavored with a mix of Indian spices and sometimes tamarind.

famous_foods.name: Kachori with Aloo Sabzi
famous_foods.description: A common breakfast or snack, kachori are deep-fried, crisp, flaky pastry often stuffed with spiced lentils or peas, served with a flavorful potato curry (aloo sabzi).

famous_foods.name: Khaja
famous_foods.description: A famous sweet dessert, Khaja is a layered pastry made from refined flour, deep-fried until crispy, and then soaked in sugar syrup, giving it a delightful sweetness and texture.

famous_foods.name: Thekua
famous_foods.description: A traditional sweet biscuit made from wheat flour, jaggery (or sugar), and clarified butter (ghee), deep-fried until golden brown. It's particularly popular during the Chhath Puja festival.

famous_foods.name: Malpua
famous_foods.description: Sweet pancakes made from flour, milk, and sometimes mashed banana or coconut, deep-fried and then dipped in sugar syrup. It's a rich and festive dessert.

famous_foods.name: Pudikiya"
famous_foods.description: Pedakiya, also known as Pudikiya in Bihar, is a traditional sweet dumpling made with refined flour and stuffed with khoya, coconut, dry fruits, and sugar. It is deep-fried until golden and crispy, commonly prepared during festivals like Holi and Diwali,

culture_tips: Dress modestly, particularly when visiting the sacred Thawe Mandir, which is a significant pilgrimage site. Shoulders and knees should generally be covered.
Always remove your footwear before entering temples and often when entering local homes as a sign of respect and cleanliness.
Show respect to elders and religious figures; greeting with a 'Namaste' (universal Indian greeting) or 'Ram Ram' (common in this region) is customary and appreciated.
